message:
perf(core): use fused rowid_and_payload_into on schema/table replay

Wire the two unconditional `cursor.rowid(cx)? + cursor.payload(cx)?`
pairs in `crates/fsqlite-core/src/compat_persist.rs` to the fused
`rowid_and_payload_into` accessor landed in 5459d778. Each fused call
reuses the parsed `CellRef` between the rowid extraction and the
payload materialisation, saving one `parse_cell_at` per row on the
schema/table replay paths.

Sites:
- compat_persist.rs:502-516 (sqlite_master replay) — pays this cost
  once per row in `sqlite_master` during file-backed schema hydration.
- compat_persist.rs:693-722 (rowid-table data replay) — pays it once
  per row of every replayed table.

Both loops now own a `payload_buf: Vec<u8>` reused across iterations
(matches the buf-reuse pattern already used elsewhere in
fsqlite-vdbe). The fused method clears+writes the buf, so the
.clear() at the top of each iteration is a no-op on the first pass
and a free reset thereafter.

Skipped: the third paired call site at engine.rs:9501-9506 only
reads the payload conditionally (inside `is_update`); fusing it would
overfetch on the non-update branch, so it stays as-is.

Parity: cargo check -p fsqlite-core clean.
Bead:   bd-9e3xf.6 follow-up to 5459d778.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
